--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2023-01-10 21:39:20.000000000) ---
Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

It looks like parallel-scale-nfs.sh is using cleanup() (via trap) at the end of the test, because it has to do extra work to unmount the NFS client mountpoint, and that is where the problem is happening, before check_and_cleanup_lustre() is called.

You might need to wrap "cleanup_nfs" in a similar manner, and then remove the cleanup() helper and leave the zconf_umount/restore_mount to check_and_cleanup_lustre().

--- Message (Charlie Olmstead, 2023-01-25 14:45:15.000000000) ---
Patch Set 11: Code-Review-1

There seems to be an edge case when a suite is skipped that's causing some odd behavior. For example mmp was skipped in this session https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/14219a6a-de86-428a-829c-d5a87d69d497
Under "Session logs" there are a lot of sanity log files, however sanity is not run for dne-part-4. dne-part-4 runs insanity, mmp, replay-ost-single, sanity-dom, sanity-flr, sanity-hsm and sanity-quota.
There aren't any subtests for mmp. I would expect to see the setup and cleanup subtests.
There are 3 test_setup and 3 test_cleanup subtests in the sanity-dom suite.

Perhaps capturing all of the setup/cleanup steps in a subtest is too difficult/messy in the Lustre Test Framework(LTF) code. I will look into the output of the LTF and see if it is cleaner for AT to handle this enhancement.

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2023-06-14 00:04:14.000000000) ---
Patch Set 44: Code-Review+1

> replay-dual timed out. The only subtest is test_setup and it is
 > marked as PASS. It seems the suite completed the setup phase and
 > then hung before the first subtest was run. This is the type of
 > failure we're trying to capture with this change.

There is LU-16863 that relates to timeouts at the start of replay-dual and other tests that are calling force_new_seq_all() because it can take a *long* time to complete (thousands of seconds).  That patch landed on 2023-04-22, so just bad timing w.r.t. this patch.

Charlie,
despite some flaws in this patch (not catching everything, sometimes duplicate startup/shutdown cases), I think it is still a net improvement for debugging, unless you saw cases where the patch actually *caused* failures?

I'd prefer to move forward with landing this patch, and then improve the cases that are being handled (e.g. fix cases with duplicate setup/cleanup if possible or distinguish them somehow, make force_new_seq_all during test startup to be its own subtest so we can track it, etc).

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2023-06-14 00:11:15.000000000) ---
Patch Set 44:

> lnet-selftest, conf-sanity and mmp did not have a setup phase:

These can be added as needed, not worse than today.

 > sanity-lnet doesn't have setup or cleanup:

Because it doesn't do Lustre setup/cleanup, not sure if we need to do anything here, but again not worse than today.

 > sanity-dom has 2 cleanup phases:

That's because sanity-dom is running sanity.sh and sanityn.sh internally, so that is probably OK.  It is pretty clear from the surrounding subtests what stage it is at.

 > performance-sanity has 3 sets of setup/cleanup, then test_6 is
 > skipped and another 3 sets of setup/cleanup:
 
I think this is the only real regression.  The subtests in here are calling other scripts that are doing the setup/cleanup, and I suspect they are somehow clobbering the other phases.

 > Perhaps capturing all of the setup/cleanup steps in a subtest is
 > too difficult/messy in the Lustre Test Framework(LTF) code. I will
 > look into the output of the LTF and see if it is cleaner for AT to
 > handle this enhancement.

If you have cycles for this, it would be great, but if not (or until that time), I think this is a net improvement.
